back_translation: After these events, Jesus traveled throughout the region of Galilee, because he did not wish to travel in the region of Judea, since the Jewish leaders were seeking a way to kill him.
At that time the Festival of Booths, which is a festival of the Jews, was approaching.
So his younger brothers said to him, "Leave this place and go to the region of Judea, so that your disciples may also see the works that you are doing.
For there is no one who wants himself to be known publicly and yet does anything in secret. Since you are doing these things, show yourself to the world."
For even his younger brothers did not yet believe in him.
Jesus therefore said to them, "My time has not yet come, but your time is always at hand.
The world cannot hate you, but it hates me, because I bear witness about the world that the works of the world are evil.
You go up to the festival. As for me, I am not yet going up to this festival, because my time has not yet been completed."
When he had said these things, he remained dwelling in the region of Galilee.
But when his younger brothers had gone up to the festival, he himself also went up — not openly, but as if in secret.
So the Jewish leaders looked for him at the festival and said, "Where is that one?"
Among the crowds there was much whispering about him. Some said, "He is a good man." But others said, "No, he leads the crowd astray instead."
However, no one spoke openly about him, because they feared the Jewish leaders.
When the festival was about half over, Jesus went up to the temple courtyard and was teaching.
So the Jewish leaders were amazed, saying, "How does this man know the Scriptures, when he has never been formally educated?"
So Jesus replied to them, "My teaching is not my own, but is from him who sent me.
If anyone wishes to do his will, that one will know whether this teaching is from God, or whether I speak on my own.
The one who speaks from himself seeks his own glory; but the one who seeks the glory of him who sent him, that one is truthful, and there is no unrighteousness in him at all.
Did not Moses give you the law? Yet none of you keeps the law. Why are you seeking to kill me?"
The crowd answered, "You have an evil spirit possessing you. Who is seeking to kill you?"
Jesus answered them, "I did one work, and you all are amazed.
For this reason Moses gave you the rite of circumcision — not that it is from Moses, but from the patriarchs who founded the lineage — and on the Sabbath you circumcise a male.
If on the Sabbath a male can be circumcised, so that the law of Moses may not be violated, why are you angry with me for making a man fully well in body on the Sabbath?
Do not judge by what the eye sees, but judge with righteous judgment."
So some of the people of Jerusalem said, "Is not this the one whom they are seeking to kill?
Look, he is speaking openly, and they say nothing to him. Have the council members really come to know that this is the Christ?
But we know where this man is from. When the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from."
While he was teaching in the temple courtyard, Jesus then cried out, "You know me, and you know where I am from. I have not come from myself, but he who sent me is true; he whom you do not know,
I know him, because I am from him, and he is the one who sent me."
So they tried to seize him, but no one laid a hand on him, because his appointed hour had not yet come.
However, many in the crowd believed in him and said, "When the Christ comes, will he do more signs than this man has done?"
When the Pharisees heard the crowd whispering these things about him, the chief priests and Pharisees sent officers to arrest him.
So Jesus said, "For a little while longer I am still with you, then I will go to him who sent me.
You will look for me, but you will not find me; and where I am, you cannot reach."
So the Jewish leaders said among themselves, "Where will he go that we will not find him? Will he go to the Jews who are dispersed among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks?
What does the saying he said mean — 'You will look for me, but you will not find me,' and 'where I am, you cannot reach'?"
On the last day of the festival, which is the most important day, Jesus stood up and cried out, "If anyone is thirsty, let that one come to me and drink.
The one who believes in me, as the Scripture has said, 'Streams of living water will flow out from within that one.'"
When he said this, he was referring to the Spirit, whom those who believed in him would later receive; for the Spirit had not yet been given, because Jesus had not yet been glorified.
When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, "This man is truly that Prophet."
Some said, "This is the Christ." But others said, "Will the Christ come from the region of Galilee?
Has not the Scripture said that the Christ will come from the lineage of David, and from Bethlehem, the village where David lived?"
So the crowd was divided because of him.
Some among them wanted to seize him, but no one laid hands on him.
So the officers returned to the chief priests and the Pharisees, who asked them, "Why did you not bring him in?"
The officers answered, "No one has ever spoken like this man."
So the Pharisees answered them, "Have you also been led astray?
Has any one of the council members or any of the Pharisees believed in him?
But this crowd that does not know the law at all — they are accursed."
Nicodemus, who had previously come to him and was one of them, said to them,
"Does our law judge any person without first hearing from that person and knowing what he has done?"
So they answered him, "Are you also from the region of Galilee? Search and see — you will find that no prophet has arisen from the region of Galilee."
[[ Then everyone went back to their own home ]]
